title: Intangibility
wikitext:
[[File:intangibility-selfe28093kitty-pryde-marvel.jpg|right|400px|[[Shadowcat|Kitty Pryde]] phasing through a wall]]

==Summary==
'''Intangibility''', in simple terms, refers to an ability or state where a character is rendered untouchable and non-physical. Characters with this ability are usually able to pass through solid matter unimpeded, and they are usually unaffected by attacks, as they pass through their bodies. The exact mechanics of intangibility differ depending on the individual, and may have to be activated in order to take effect. Additionally, characters may not be able to interact with physical objects when intangible.

There are numerous forms of intangibility, and intangibility users should preferably have their method of intangibility specified on their page, as the workings can be countered through different means, depending on the type. Also of note, the various forms of physical intangibility are not automatically assumed to function under a hierarchy; thus, [[Non-Physical Interaction]] in regards to interacting with these types of intangibility is to be primarily based on feats unless a verse depicts the contrary. For example, a character with feats of interacting with Non-Physical Intangibility is not assumed to be able to interact with Elemental Intangibility if they do not have feats of doing so.

==Types==
===<u>Elemental Intangibility</u>===

'''Elemental Intangibility''' is the ability to transform into various elements, substances, and/or states of matter to achieve a form of pseudo-intangibility. Rather than being truly intangible, Elemental Intangibility involves the user possessing the ability to transform their body into a specific element in order to avoid physical damage as attacks merely disperse the user's body or pass harmlessly through it without truly damaging them. Unlike true intangibility, Elemental Intangibility does not grant the ability to pass unimpeded through solid matter, though depending on the element the user can turn into they still may be able to move through certain materials. Otherwise, they are assumed to physically exist within the physical world and can be damaged as such, and depending on the element, [[Non-Physical Interaction]] may not be needed in order to physically interact with or damage them.
*'''Type 1 (Solids):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into a mass of solid matter or solid particles, such as sand, ice, paper, or plants, or even non-natural materials such as nanomachines or small creatures such as insects. Due to still being made of solid physical matter, characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility can still usually be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Crocodile (One Piece)|Crocodile]], [[Sandman (Marvel Comics)|Sandman]], [[Kuzan (Aokiji)|Aokiji]], [[Konan]]
*'''Type 2 (Semi-Solids/Semi-Liquids):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into a mass of semi-solid or semi-liquid matter, such as mud, lava/magma, liquid metal, or any other non-Newtonian substance. Rather than acting as a true liquid or true solid, this intangibility exists in-between the two. This is primarily determined by its [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viscosity#Order_of_magnitude_estimates Viscosity Value], with anything with a factor of 10^1 or higher falling into this category rather than a true liquid. Due to being primarily or partially made of physical matter, characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility can still usually be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Sakazuki (Akainu)|Akainu]], [[Charlotte Katakuri]], [[T-1000]]
*'''Type 3 (Liquids):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into liquid matter, such as water, blood, or any other Newtonian liquid. This is primarily determined by its [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viscosity#Order_of_magnitude_estimates Viscosity Value], with anything with a factor of 10^0 or lower falling into this category. Due to being partially made of physical matter, characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility can still usually be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Juvia Lockser]], [[Evil Natural Water]], [[Olhydra]]
*'''Type 4 (Aerosols):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into a state of semi-gaseous, semi-liquid or semi-solid liquid matter, such as mist/fog, smoke, dust, or ash. Rather than acting as a true liquid or true solid, this intangibility exists in-between a liquid and a gaseous state as macroscopic to microscopic particles of liquid or solid matter suspended within a gas. This is primarily determined by its [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Particulate_matter#Size,_shape,_and_solubility_matter Particulate Matter scale], with anything with a scale of 10 micrometers or less, but above the scale of molecules, falling into this category rather than a true gas. Characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Smoker (One Piece)|Smoker]], [[Suika Ibuki]]
*'''Type 5 (Gas):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into a state of true gas, such as steam, or any non-specific matter on a molecular or atomic scale. Users of this type of intangibility differ from Type 4 due to existing in a much smaller state of matter, being intangible on a molecular or atomic scale rather than just microscopic. Characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Caesar Clown]], [[Ruby Rose]]
*'''Type 6 (Plasma):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into plasma, the most common examples being lightning and fire. This state of matter exists on a similar scale to Type 5, with the major difference being its existence in a higher energy state than gas, consisting primarily of charged particles (ions and/or electrons) rather than neutral particles. Characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Portgas D. Ace]], [[Enel]], [[Raiden Shogun (Genshin Impact)|Raiden Shogun]], [[Electro (Marvel Comics)|Electro]], [[Vox]]
*'''Type 7 (Quantum Particles):''' The user is able to become intangible by breaking themselves down into a particle state, existing as sub-atomic particles such as photons, electrons, and quarks. Characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction.
**'''Examples:''' [[Borsalino (Kizaru)|Kizaru]], [[Delsin Rowe]]
*'''Type 8 (Energy):''' The user is able to become intangible by becoming pure energy. Due to completely lacking any form of physical matter and existing solely as an energy state in the physical world, characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by any physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction. This form of intangibility is nearly identical mechanically to Non-Physical intangibility, and often overlaps with it.
**'''Examples:''' [[Pyron]]
*'''Type 9 (Non-Physical):''' The user is able to become intangible by turning into an element which has no natural energy and no physical presence in the material world, the most common example being shadows/darkness. Due to completely lacking any form of physical matter, as well as lacking an energy state in the physical world, characters with this type of Elemental Intangibility usually cannot be damaged by any physical means without the use of Non-Physical Interaction. This form of intangibility is nearly identical mechanically to Non-Physical intangibility, and often overlaps with it.
**'''Examples:''' [[Rogue Cheney]], [[Xiang Tzu]]

===<u>Physical Intangibility</u>===

'''Physical Intangibility''' is a state of true intangibility, as the user is rendered fully untouchable and non-physical. Users of this category of Intangibility are intangible on a physical level, meaning they are assumed to exist within the physical world to some degree. [[Non-Physical Interaction]] is usually required to be able to interact with or harm characters with these forms of intangibility.
*'''Non-Physical:''' The user has a non-physical form, a "body" that by nature lacks physical material or substance. This type of intangibility allows the user to pass through solid matter and attacks due to not being made up of a physical substance, such as ghosts or characters who merely exist as a disembodied soul. It also includes characters that are made up of things like energy.
**'''Users:''' [[Lille Barro]] ([[Bleach]]), [[Danny Phantom]] ([[Danny Phantom (The Universe)|Danny Phantom]])
*'''Molecular:''' The ability to become intangible by manipulating one's own physical matter in order to phase through solid objects. This is most commonly done through various applications of speed, by which the user is able to vibrate their own molecules at high speeds to become intangible, but it can also be achieved through abilities such as quantum tunneling and density manipulation. However, this does not necessarily allow the user to phase through energy-based attacks.
**'''Users:''' [[Flash (Wally West) (Post-Crisis)|Flash]] and [[Superman (Post-Crisis)|Superman]] ([[DC Comics]]), [[Shadowcat]] and [[Vision (Marvel Comics)|Vision]] ([[Marvel Comics]])
*'''Dimensional:''' The ability allows users to shift in and out of material tangibility by shifting between different dimensional variances, essentially jumping between a subspace connecting the one world they occupy with an adjacent one it is saddled by, enabling one easy interaction with both while not necessarily being situated within either. They may also be able to do this by way of shifting between higher or lower spatial dimensions. While this allows them to avoid most forms of attack, they can still be affected by attacks that operate on similar principles and by those with sufficient range to target them in the other dimension.
**'''Users:''' [[Obito Uchiha]] ([[Naruto (Verse)|Naruto]]), [[Reimu Hakurei]] ([[Touhou Project]]), [[Ahzek Ahriman]] ([[Warhammer 40,000]])
